title: Creamy German Green Pepper Dumplings
description: A traditional German dish featuring green peppers and a creamy sauce, steamed to perfection in a leaf wrap, this recipe is vegetarian friendly and perfect for a monsoon day. The resinous and gooey texture of the dumplings is sure to delight.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, and black pepper.
Gradually add the vegetable oil and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place a green pepper slice in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the pepper to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining dough and peppers.
In a large pot, bring 2 inches of water to a boil and reduce the heat to a simmer.
Place a steamer basket over the pot and add the dumplings.
Cover the pot with a lid and steam the dumplings for 15 minutes.
While the dumplings are steaming, heat the sour cream and grated cheese in a saucepan over low heat until the cheese is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Serve the dumplings hot with the creamy sauce spooned over the top and garnished with chopped parsley.
